perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Formation of the Cooperation Channel (a) During the term of this Agreement, Party B will use the Baidu's Real Estate and Home Furnishing Channel as the jointly formed channel of Party A and Party B. Party A grants all-round exclusive rights to Party B to construct, maintain and operate the Cooperation channel. As the owner of www.baidu.com, Party A has ownership and control over second tier domain names. Party A has the right to deprive Party B of the operating rights of the website without any liability if Party B is found in violation of any law. Party A is required to receive written consent from Party B prior to its adjustment of any second tier domain names which is under independent operation of Party B.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Formation of the Cooperation Channel (a) During the term of this Agreement, Party B will use the Baidu's Real Estate and Home Furnishing Channel as the jointly formed channel of Party A and Party B. Party A grants all-round exclusive rights to Party B to construct, maintain and operate the Cooperation channel. As the owner of www.baidu.com, Party A has ownership and control over second tier domain names. Party A has the right to deprive Party B of the operating rights of the website with a 30-day written notice if Party B is found in violation of any law. Party A is NOT required to receive written consent from Party B prior to its adjustment of any second tier domain names which is under independent operation of Party B.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering Party A's rights regarding the Cooperation Channel. Previously, Party A could deprive Party B of operating rights without liability if Party B violated any law, but now requires a 30-day written notice. Further, it removes the requirement for Party A to obtain written consent from Party B before adjusting second-tier domain names. This contradicts the earlier grant of 'all-round exclusive rights' to Party B and creates uncertainty about the level of control each party has over the channel. The contradiction lies in the fact that Party B is granted exclusive rights, but Party A can now make adjustments to the domain names without Party B's consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Formation of the Cooperation Channel (a) During the term of this Agreement, Party B will use the Baidu's Real Estate and Home Furnishing Channel as the jointly formed channel of Party A and Party B. Party A grants all-round exclusive rights to Party B to construct, maintain and operate the Cooperation channel. As the owner of www.baidu.com, Party A has ownership and control over second tier domain names. Party A has the right to deprive Party B of the operating rights of the website with a 30-day written notice if Party B is found in violation of any law. Party A is NOT required to receive written consent from Party B prior to its adjustment of any second tier domain names which is under independent operation of Party B.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction regarding Party B's 'all-round exclusive rights.' Previously, Party A needed written consent for adjustments to second-tier domain names. Now, Party A is NOT required to obtain written consent, which directly conflicts with the grant of exclusive rights to Party B to construct, maintain and operate the Cooperation channel. The contradiction lies in the fact that Party A grants exclusive rights, but now makes adjustments to the domain names without Party B's consent. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties. This creates in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.'

perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE VII EXCLUSIVITY During the term of this Agreement, Party B and its affiliates will be the exclusive cooperator of Party A's real estate and home furnishing cooperation channel. Party B and its affiliates will be the exclusive provider of real estate and home furnishing information, products and data in Party A's real estate and home furnishing channel. Party A may not make any identical or similar cooperation regarding the real estate and home furnishing information, products and data in its real estate and home furnishing channel with any competitor of Party B.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE VII EXCLUSIVITY During the term of this Agreement, Party B and its affiliates will be the exclusive cooperator of Party A's real estate and home furnishing cooperation channel, except in cases where Party A determines that cooperation with another party would provide significant additional value. Party B and its affiliates will be the exclusive provider of real estate and home furnishing information, products and data in Party A's real estate and home furnishing channel. Party A may, at its discretion, make similar cooperation regarding the real estate and home furnishing information, products and data in its real estate and home furnishing channel with any competitor of Party B, provided it doesn't exceed 20% of the channel's overall content.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding exceptions to the exclusivity clause. Initially, Party B was the exclusive cooperator and provider, and Party A was prohibited from cooperating with Party B's competitors. Now, Party A can cooperate with competitors under certain conditions (significant additional value or if it doesn't exceed 20% of the content). This directly contradicts the original exclusivity clause, creating ambiguity about the extent of Party B's exclusive rights. It creates an in-text contradiction with the sentence 'Party A may not make any identical or similar cooperation regarding the real estate and home furnishing information, products and data in its real estate and home furnishing channel with any competitor of Party B.', since that statement is no longer true.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE VII EXCLUSIVITY During the term of this Agreement, Party B and its affiliates will be the exclusive cooperator of Party A's real estate and home furnishing cooperation channel, except in cases where Party A determines that cooperation with another party would provide significant additional value. Party B and its affiliates will be the exclusive provider of real estate and home furnishing information, products and data in Party A's real estate and home furnishing channel. Party A may, at its discretion, make similar cooperation regarding the real estate and home furnishing information, products and data in its real estate and home furnishing channel with any competitor of Party B, provided it doesn't exceed 20% of the channel's overall content.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibited Party A from cooperating with any competitor of Party B. The changed text now allows Party A to cooperate with competitors under certain conditions. The contradicted text is the changed text itself. The sentence 'Party A may not make any identical or similar cooperation regarding the real estate and home furnishing information, products and data in its real estate and home furnishing channel with any competitor of Party B' is directly contradicted by the addition of clauses that allow similar cooperation under specific conditions. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, specifically when different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.
